title: Eucarpha strobilina
intro:
There is not much information available on Eucarpha strobilina, however I found some information about a different plant which is Eucalyptus strobilina. 

Eucalyptus strobilina, also known as the strobilanthe or Strobilanthes strobilina, is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae, commonly known as the acanthus or thistle family. 

Eucalyptus strobilina has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, wounds, and other infections. 

The bioactive constituents of Eucalyptus strobilina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. 

The most common herbal preparations made with Eucalyptus strobilina include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis, and skin conditions, such as acne and wounds.
taxonomy: Eucarpha strobilina is classified as a plant species in the family Proteaceae. The Proteaceae family is a group of flowering plants native to the Southern Hemisphere, particularly in Australia, New Zealand, and South Africa.
